name: Top of the South (TOTS)
description: A challenging top-of-the-south mission with long climbs and changing conditions.
text1: Top of the South (TOTS) is a hard bikepacking route covering about 1,106 km, commonly ridden over 5 days. A challenging top-of-the-south mission with long climbs and changing conditions. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Nov-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Pelorus and Queen Charlotte singletrack arrives near the end when legs are already heavy from days of forestry climbing. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
text2: Steep forestry climbs, weather volatility, and remote links combine into a demanding multi-day route. Riders should be confident with backcountry decision-making and long effort blocks.
text3: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer. For this 5-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
